summary: Research on the dwarf haploid parthenosporophyte *Tauya basicrassa* (Phaeophyceae, Laminariales) from the Sea of Okhotsk, Russia, has established this alga as a novel laminariacean species through integrated morphological, molecular, and chromosomal analyses. The studies revealed that *T. basicrassa* exhibits a unique genetic profile, characterized by 100% sequence identity in plastid-encoded RuBisCo genes alongside a distinct nuclear rDNA sequence, confirming its status as a parthenosporophyte. Significant morphological differences between the dwarf and normal forms indicate a natural evolutionary divergence, highlighting the endemic and genetically distinct nature of this species within the cold-water ecosystems of the Sea of Okhotsk.
